perturbation.original_text: BOSCH hereby grants CLIENT un-exclusive "Reserved Rights" to sell and distribute the "Product" within the "Territory".
perturbation.changed_text: BOSCH hereby revokes all un-exclusive "Reserved Rights" to sell and distribute the "Product" within the "Territory".
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction. Section 5 states that BOSCH grants CLIENT "un-exclusive Reserved Rights." This perturbation revokes it in the same section. This creates an in-text contradiction because it is unclear whether the client has these rights or not, creating uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: RIGHTS GRANTED: BOSCH hereby grants CLIENT the "Exclusive Distribution License Rights" sell and distribute the Products within the "Territory". BOSCH hereby revokes all un-exclusive "Reserved Rights" to sell and distribute the "Product" within the "Territory".
perturbation.justification: The original text grants un-exclusive rights, while the changed text revokes those same un-exclusive rights. The contradicted text states 'BOSCH hereby grants CLIENT the "Exclusive Distribution License Rights" sell and distribute the Products within the "Territory". BOSCH hereby revokes all un-exclusive "Reserved Rights" to sell and distribute the "Product" within the "Territory".' Because both sections grant and revoke rights, which directly contradicts the other, there is an in-text contradiction, creating uncertainty about the client's rights and creating ambiguity about whether the client has rights or not.

perturbation.original_text: CLIENT will not exercise nor authorize any other Party to to take any action which would reasonably be deemed to derogate from, impair or compete with the Exclusive Rights, Reserved rights, and Rights Granted herein granted.
perturbation.changed_text: CLIENT will exercise and authorize other Parties to take any action which would reasonably be deemed to derogate from, impair or compete with the Exclusive Rights, Reserved rights, and Rights Granted herein granted.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction in section 8. Originally CLIENT could not take action to undermine the exclusive rights of BOSCH, but now the CLIENT can directly undermine the rights of BOSCH as this clause now states CLIENT will exercise and authorize other Parties to take action. This in-text contradiction generates uncertainty in how the agreement should be interpreted, leading to possible disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: RESERVED RIGHTS: All business, locations, entities, people, etc... without any limitations. This is in concert and approval with BOSCH and is limited to any and all of BOSCH's current clients. CLIENT will exercise and authorize other Parties to take any action which would reasonably be deemed to derogate from, impair or compete with the Exclusive Rights, Reserved rights, and Rights Granted herein granted. (i) COST OF PRODUCT: Cost is based upon square inch and reserved confidentially. (ii) COST OF PRODUCT TO MARKET: The established price for the market place will be negotiated confidentially but will follow the max and min limitations allowed. Special pricing for large orders and/or custom orders will need written approval (email) from BOSCH. (iv) COST AND EXPENSES FOR CLIENT: All Costs, Marketing, sales reports/delivery, sales staff, office locations and expense as well as miscellaneous delivery costs accrued by CLIENT (including its subsidiaries and affiliates) by reason of, in connection with the products is the sole responsibility of CLIENT. All manufacturing and delivery will be the responsibility of BOSCH. CLIENT is responsible for shipping and delivery cost of product to end user. (v) ASSISTANCE TO CLIENT: In addition to its other obligations under this Agreement, BOSCH shall assist CLIENT in obtaining (and provide all documentation required to obtain) such licenses and/or permits as may be necessary or desirable for the sales and/or Distribution and/or Placement of the Product for city, state and/or federal contract accounts (Exit signs in and on buildings, mandatory safety fixtures, freeway signs, etc...) within the Territory. 1
perturbation.justification: The original text prevented the CLIENT from undermining BOSCH's rights, while the changed text allows the CLIENT to actively undermine those rights. This directly contradicts the section RESERVED RIGHTS, which outlines specific rights and responsibilities that are now undermined by the CLIENT's ability to derogate from, impair, or compete with BOSCH's rights. The phrase 'CLIENT will exercise and authorize other Parties to take any action which would reasonably be deemed to derogate from, impair or compete with the Exclusive Rights, Reserved rights, and Rights Granted herein granted' in both the Changed Text and Contradicted Text highlights the direct conflict, making it a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: CLIENT is responsible for shipping and delivery cost of product to end user.
perturbation.changed_text: BOSCH is responsible for shipping and delivery cost of product to end user.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction in section 8. This change shifts shipping responsibility to BOSCH, directly contradicting the previous sentence that holds the CLIENT responsible for these costs. This in-text contradiction creates ambiguity about which party is responsible for shipping costs, affecting the financial obligations of each party under the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: RESERVED RIGHTS: All business, locations, entities, people, etc... without any limitations. This is in concert and approval with BOSCH and is limited to any and all of BOSCH's current clients. CLIENT will not exercise nor authorize any other Party to to take any action which would reasonably be deemed to derogate from, impair or compete with the Exclusive Rights, Reserved rights, and Rights Granted herein granted. (i) COST OF PRODUCT: Cost is based upon square inch and reserved confidentially. (ii) COST OF PRODUCT TO MARKET: The established price for the market place will be negotiated confidentially but will follow the max and min limitations allowed. Special pricing for large orders and/or custom orders will need written approval (email) from BOSCH. (iv) COST AND EXPENSES FOR CLIENT: All Costs, Marketing, sales reports/delivery, sales staff, office locations and expense as well as miscellaneous delivery costs accrued by CLIENT (including its subsidiaries and affiliates) by reason of, in connection with the products is the sole responsibility of CLIENT. All manufacturing and delivery will be the responsibility of BOSCH. BOSCH is responsible for shipping and delivery cost of product to end user. (v) ASSISTANCE TO CLIENT: In addition to its other obligations under this Agreement, BOSCH shall assist CLIENT in obtaining (and provide all documentation required to obtain) such licenses and/or permits as may be necessary or desirable for the sales and/or Distribution and/or Placement of the Product for city, state and/or federal contract accounts (Exit signs in and on buildings, mandatory safety fixtures, freeway signs, etc...) within the Territory. 1
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the CLIENT is responsible for shipping costs, while the changed text shifts this responsibility to BOSCH. The contradicted text within section (iv) 'COST AND EXPENSES FOR CLIENT' explicitly states '...miscellaneous delivery costs accrued by CLIENT (including its subsidiaries and affiliates) by reason of, in connection with the products is the sole responsibility of CLIENT. All manufacturing and delivery will be the responsibility of BOSCH. BOSCH is responsible for shipping and delivery cost of product to end user.' This change creates a direct contradiction about which party bears the shipping costs, potentially leading to disputes and impacting financial obligations outlined in the contract. Therefore, the condition for a strong in-text contradiction exists.
